task_completion.visual_evidence: The user profile in the top right corner shows 'Admin User' or the admin dashboard is visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the role creation page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The page title or breadcrumbs show 'Define roles' or 'Add a new role' under Site administration.
A dropdown or selection field for 'Use role or archetype' shows 'Non-editing teacher' selected.

task_completion.description: Set the custom full name and short name
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text input fields for 'Custom full name' and 'Short name' contain 'Lab Assistant' and 'labassistant' respectively.

task_completion.description: Set the context types where the role may be assigned
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for 'Course' and 'Activity module' are checked in the 'Context types where this role may be assigned' section.
The capability list shows 'Manage activities' (moodle/course:manageactivities) with the 'Allow' radio button or checkbox selected.

integrity.description: Agent used the Moodle web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Moodle web application in a browser, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to mock answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into the actual Moodle form fields, not written in a text editor or notes app.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The Moodle interface appears fully functional with standard navigation menus, blocks, and styling.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom role must have the shortname 'labassistant', fullname 'Lab Assistant', and the capability 'moodle/course:manageactivities' set to Allow. The archetype must be 'Non-editing teacher' (internally 'teacher').
